key_metrics_output: particle count, polymer type (PA, PET, PP, PVF, PE, PS, fiberglass), particle shape (film, fragment, fiber, granule, foam), color, size distribution, deposition rate (particles/m²/day for passive sampling; particles/m³ for active sampling)
abstract: This critical review analyzes atmospheric microplastics (MPs) sampling methods and standardization requirements based on 92 articles published between 2015 and 2023. The review examines passive and active sampling approaches, comparing sampling tools, time settings, locations, sources, and intake flow rates. Key findings indicate that atmospheric MPs pose serious threats to ecosystems and human health through inhalation exposure and long-distance atmospheric transport. The review emphasizes that non-standardized sampling methods significantly limit data comparability and credibility, and calls for establishment of globally standardized sampling protocols to ensure accuracy and consistency in atmospheric MPs research.
